Document:
# CAPITAL CRIMES All capital crimes are punished by: - Permanent confinement - Revocation of weapon licenses - Removal from public office or security authority - Possible off-world transfer or exile (if approved by the proper authority) Execution remains illegal.
## Murder Killing another person with malicious intent.
## Attempted Murder Attempting to kill another person with malicious intent.
## Mass Casualty Sabotage Damaging colony systems in a way that could cause mass death or colony collapse (reactor/atmospheric/water system sabotage, disabling evacuation systems, disabling quarantine controls, opening colony defenses during hostile contact).
## Terrorism Using violence, sabotage, hostage-taking, threats, or mass panic to coerce the colony, government, or civilian population.
## Sedition Attempting to violently overthrow or replace the lawful colonial government or civil administration. Includes armed revolt against lawful colony authority during an emergency.
## Crimes Against Humanity Heinous acts against human life or dignity (torture, forced infection, unlawful human experimentation, intentional exposure to dangerous organisms, biological weapon deployment, sacrificing civilians to preserve corporate or scientific assets, knowingly allowing a preventable outbreak for profit or concealment).
## Jailbreak / Escape Escaping, attempting escape, or assisting escape from lawful confinement. - Permanent confinement (major/capital offenders) | 20 min + original sentence (minor offenders)
## Use of Prohibited Weapons Using explosives, chemical/biological/radiological weapons, or engineered pathogens against people or colony assets.
## Specimen Trafficking Selling, concealing, or attempting to export restricted biological material without lawful authorization.
